output.summary: Visual QA PASS. The Tabs block implementation matches the Figma design across all verified breakpoints. Desktop layout (tablist + panel), mobile accordion, per-tab color feature, and typography all match the design intent. Colors differ from Figma because the implementation correctly uses theme accent tokens rather than hardcoded hex values. Cross-browser rendering is consistent across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit.

observations: Overall shape and proportions match Figma across desktop and mobile
Major elements (eyebrow, heading, description, buttons, tablist, panel) are all in correct positions
Relative sizes of tab buttons vs panel are correct (50/50 split)
Visual weight and density match -- tab buttons have appropriate min-height (~140px), panel fills remaining space
No content overflow or clipping observed
All interactive elements (tab buttons, accordion summaries, CTAs) are visible and accessible

1440.layout: Desktop tablist on left + panel on right
observations: Two-column header layout with heading left, description + buttons right -- matches Figma
Tab buttons left-bordered with rounded-left corners (rounded-l-3xl ~24px vs Figma 25px) -- within 1px tolerance
Active tab shows accent fill + description text visible -- matches Figma active state
Inactive tabs have border only, no fill -- matches Figma
Panel has rounded corners (~24px), lighter accent background, 24px padding -- matches Figma
Panel shows tab title + body content -- matches Figma
Tab button min-height ~140px -- matches Figma specification
Gap between tab buttons ~16px -- matches Figma gap/gap-3
Tablist has 64px vertical padding -- matches Figma margin/margin-5

1024.layout: Desktop tablist on left + panel on right
observations: Same two-column tab layout as 1440px but narrower
Tab buttons and panel scale proportionally
Typography and spacing remain correct
No overflow or crowding observed
Switches to accordion (details/summary) pattern at this width
Full border around each accordion item with rounded corners (rounded-3xl ~24px) -- matches Figma mobile rounded-[25px]
Plus/minus icons for expand/collapse -- matches Figma
No Figma reference at this breakpoint; accordion behavior is appropriate
Buttons stack vertically -- appropriate for width
Accordion items with full border and rounded corners -- matches Figma mobile design
Tab titles use Anton font at appropriate mobile size -- matches Figma font/size/2xl (32px)
Plus (+) icon for collapsed items, minus (-) for expanded -- matches Figma
Expanded accordion item shows accent background fill -- matches Figma active state concept
Content renders inline within expanded accordion -- matches Figma mobile structure
Horizontal padding (18px) appropriate for mobile -- matches Figma margin/margin-3

details: Eyebrow: Uppercase, small size (~14px), medium weight -- matches Figma Others/Eyebrow style
Block heading: Anton Regular, large size (56px desktop, 40px mobile) -- matches Figma Headline/H3
Description: Regular weight, 16px, line-height 1.5 -- matches Figma Body/Body - Medium
Tab button titles: Anton, ~40px desktop -- matches Figma Headline/H5 (font/size/3xl)
Tab descriptions: Regular weight, 16px -- matches Figma body style
Panel heading: Anton, ~40px -- matches Figma
Button text: Uppercase, medium weight, 16px -- matches Figma Others/Button
Anton font appears slanted by design but is NOT italic -- correctly implemented without italic class

Active tab button: Uses bg-accent (theme token) -- Figma shows #FFD24C (gold) in Desktop 1, #47CBF2 (blue) in Desktop 2, #F26D45 (orange) in Mobile. Implementation correctly uses CSS custom properties that adapt per-page/per-tab.
Panel background: Uses bg-accent-lighter (theme token) -- Figma shows #FFEDB7 (light gold) in Desktop 1, #B5EAFA (light blue) in Desktop 2. Implementation correctly uses lighter variant of accent.
Text color: Dark charcoal (#1f1f1d) throughout -- matches Figma
Border color: Charcoal -- matches Figma
Per-tab color feature: Correctly overrides --accent-color CSS custom property per tab, changing both button fill and panel background. Verified with orange, green, blue, and purple colors.
Section padding: py-10 (40px) mobile, sm:py-16 (64px) desktop -- matches Figma margin/margin-5 (64px desktop), margin/margin-4 (24px mobile padding-y)
Container gap: gap-y-10 (40px) mobile, sm:gap-y-16 (64px) desktop -- matches Figma gap/gap-8 (64px)
Tab button gap: gap-4 (16px) -- matches Figma gap/gap-3 (16px)
Tab button padding: p-6 (24px) -- matches Figma margin/margin-2 (24px)
Panel padding: p-6 (24px) -- matches Figma margin/margin-2 (24px)
Tablist vertical padding: py-16 (64px) -- matches Figma margin/margin-5 (64px)
Heading gap: gap-5 (32px) -- matches Figma gap/gap-5 (32px)
Tab buttons: rounded-l-3xl (~24px left corners) -- Figma shows 25px, within 1px tolerance
Panel: rounded-3xl (~24px) -- Figma shows 25px, within 1px tolerance
Mobile accordion items: rounded-3xl (~24px) -- Figma shows 25px, within 1px tolerance
CTA buttons: Pill shape with large radius -- matches Figma 119px rounded
Desktop: Horizontal layout with tablist (flex-1) and panel (flex-1) -- matches Figma 50/50 split
Tablist: Vertical flex column with gap -- matches Figma vertical tab arrangement
Panel: Stretches to match tablist height -- matches Figma
Mobile: Vertical accordion layout -- matches Figma mobile
Header: 2-column layout at desktop (heading left, description+buttons right) -- matches Figma
Header: Stacks vertically on mobile -- appropriate responsive behavior
Tab button text: Left-aligned with vertical centering -- matches Figma
Panel content: Top-left aligned -- matches Figma
Eyebrow: Left-aligned -- matches Figma
All elements properly aligned within their containers

observations: Default (no color): Uses page accent color for active button and accent-lighter for panel -- working correctly
Blue tab: Active button shows blue, panel shows light blue -- verified via functional QA screenshots
Orange tab: Active button shows orange, panel shows peach/salmon -- verified via tabs-percolor-orange-v2-1440w.png
Green tab: Active button shows green, panel shows light green -- verified via tabs-percolor-mixed-1440w.png
Purple tab: Active button shows purple, panel shows light purple -- verified via functional QA
Color transition on tab switch: JS properly removes old color class and applies new one -- confirmed with delayed screenshots
Mobile accordion: Per-tab colors applied to details elements when open -- verified via mobile screenshots
Description reveal animation: Active tab description slides in with max-h/opacity transition -- smooth
All text has sufficient contrast against backgrounds (dark charcoal on light accent colors)
Active tab has clear visual distinction from inactive tabs (filled vs border-only)
Focus states available on tab buttons (cursor-pointer set, keyboard navigation verified by functional QA)
Mobile accordion expand/collapse state clearly indicated by plus/minus icon
All three browsers render identically at all four breakpoints (375, 768, 1024, 1440)
No font rendering differences observed
No layout shifts or border-radius inconsistencies
Accordion behavior consistent across browsers

notes.observation: Figma designs show specific pillar colors (gold #FFD24C, blue #47CBF2, orange #F26D45) while the implementation uses theme accent tokens (--accent-color). This is by design -- the theme's accent color system allows per-page color customization, which is the correct architectural approach. The per-tab color feature extends this to allow individual tabs to override the accent.

The desktop/mobile breakpoint appears to be at approximately 960px (60rem in JS) for the interactive behavior, while CSS md: breakpoint is at 768px. At 768px, the accordion pattern is displayed (mobile mode). At 1024px, the full tablist+panel desktop layout is shown. This is intentional -- the two-column layout requires sufficient width.
Border radius is 24px (rounded-3xl) in implementation vs 25px in Figma. The 1px difference is within the 2-4px tolerance.
